client_like.c(3) Library Functions Manual client_like.c(3)

NAME

client_like.c - Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


terminal_fd_reader_t * session_client_like_get_stdin_reader (void)
asciichat_error_t session_client_like_run (const session_client_like_config_t *config)

Variables


bool(*)(void *) session_client_like_get_render_should_exit (void)

Detailed Description

Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes.

Definition in file client_like.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t session_client_like_run (const session_client_like_config_t * config)

Run a client-like mode with fully shared initialization and teardown

This function orchestrates the complete lifecycle of client-like modes:

Initialization (automatic from GET_OPTION())

Terminal:

  • Forces stderr when stdout is piped (terminal_should_force_stderr)
  • Disables terminal logging during rendering, re-enables on exit
  • Handles snapshot mode log visibility

Keepawake:

  • Validates --keepawake / --no-keepawake mutual exclusivity
  • Calls platform_enable_keepawake() unless --no-keepawake set
  • Disables keepawake on exit

Splash Screen:

  • Creates temporary display context
  • Calls splash_intro_start() to show splash animation
  • Holds splash visible during media probing and audio init
  • Calls splash_intro_done() when capture and display are ready

Media / Webcam (session_capture_ctx_t):

•
Selects source type from options (priority order):
1.
--url → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE (FFmpeg handles http/rtsp/rtmp)
2.
--file → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE; "-" → MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN
3.
--test-pattern → MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST
4.
(default) → MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM
  • FPS: uses GET_OPTION(fps) if > 0, otherwise probes with temporary media source
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp) as initial seek
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_loop) for file looping
  • Calls session_capture_create() to open webcam or media file
  • Destroys capture on exit

Audio (audio_context_t):

  • Skipped entirely for immediate snapshots (snapshot_mode && snapshot_delay == 0)
  • Probes for audio via media_source_has_audio() on capture's source
  • Allocates and initializes audio_context_t when audio available
  • Links audio context to capture and media source
  • Sets playback_only based on audio_should_enable_microphone()
  • Disables jitter buffering for local file playback
  • Calls audio_start_duplex() after splash_intro_done()
  • Stops, destroys, and frees audio on exit

Display (session_display_ctx_t):

  • Calls session_display_create() with snapshot and color settings
  • Passes audio context if initialized
  • Destroys display on exit

Mode-Specific Loop

Calls config->run_fn(capture, display, config->run_user_data) once all initialization is complete. This is where each mode calls session_render_loop() or runs its own protocol-thread-driven loop.

Cleanup (always runs, even on error)

Cleanup sequence (order is critical):

1.
Re-enable terminal logging
2.
audio_terminate_portaudio_final() - Free PortAudio device resources FIRST
3.
audio_stop_duplex() + audio_destroy() + SAFE_FREE
4.
session_display_destroy()
5.
session_capture_destroy()
6.
webcam_destroy() - Free cached webcam images and test patterns
7.
session_log_buffer_destroy()
8.
platform_disable_keepawake()
9.
Print '

Parameters

Config Module Mode configuration. Must not be NULL. run_fn must not be NULL.

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, or the first error code from initialization or from run_fn. Cleanup always runs regardless of return value.

Definition at line 96 of file client_like.c.

96                                                                                       {
97   log_info("session_client_like_run: ENTRY");
98   if (!config || !config->run_fn) {
99     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config or run_fn is NULL");
100   }
101   log_info("session_client_like_run: config validated");
102 
103   // Store config globally for adapter callbacks
104   g_current_config = config;
105 
106   // Store the render should_exit adapter globally so mode-specific run_fn can access it
107   g_render_should_exit = display_should_exit_adapter;
108 
109   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
110 
111   // Keep track of what's been initialized for cleanup
112   session_capture_ctx_t *capture = NULL;
113   session_display_ctx_t *display = NULL;
114   audio_context_t *audio_ctx = NULL;
115   media_source_t *probe_source = NULL;
116   bool audio_available = false;
117 
118   // ============================================================================
119   // SETUP: Terminal and Logging
120   // ============================================================================
121 
122   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up terminal and logging");
123 
124   // Force stderr when stdout is piped (prevents ASCII corruption in output stream)
125   bool should_force_stderr = terminal_should_force_stderr();
126   log_debug("terminal_should_force_stderr()=%d", should_force_stderr);
127 
128   if (should_force_stderr) {
129     // Redirect logs to stderr to prevent corruption of stdout (for pipes)
130     // But keep terminal output enabled so we can see initialization errors
131     log_set_force_stderr(true);
132   }
133 
134   // Note: Force all logging to stderr if encoding to pipe to prevent corrupting binary video output
135   // (implemented via should_force_stderr check above)
136   const char *render_file_early = GET_OPTION(render_file);
137   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_RUN] render_file_early=%s (ptr=%p)",
138            render_file_early ? render_file_early : "(null)", (void *)render_file_early);
139   if (render_file_early && render_file_early[0] != '\0' &&
140       (strcmp(render_file_early, "-") == 0 || strcmp(render_file_early, "pipe:") == 0)) {
141     log_info("--render-file=- will output binary video to stdout, logging sent to stderr");
142   }
143 
144   // ============================================================================
145   // SETUP: Keepawake System
146   // ============================================================================
147 
148   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Validating keepawake options");
149 
150   // Validate mutual exclusivity
151   bool enable_ka = GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake);
152   bool disable_ka = GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake);
153   log_debug("enable_keepawake=%d, disable_keepawake=%d", enable_ka, disable_ka);
154 
155   if (enable_ka && disable_ka) {
156     result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "--keepawake and --no-keepawake are mutually exclusive");
157     goto cleanup;
158   }
159 
160   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Enabling keepawake if needed");
161 
162   // Enable keepawake unless explicitly disabled
163   if (!disable_ka) {
164     (void)platform_enable_keepawake();
165   }
166 
167   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Keepawake setup complete");
168 
169   // ============================================================================
170   // SETUP: Display Context (before splash for unified TTY initialization)
171   // ============================================================================
172 
173   // Create display context early to share TTY initialization between splash and rendering
174   // This prevents double TTY init which was corrupting the terminal state
175   session_display_config_t display_config = {0};
176   display_config.snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
177   display_config.palette_type = GET_OPTION(palette_type);
178   display_config.custom_palette = GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set) ? GET_OPTION(palette_custom) : NULL;
179   display_config.color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO;
180   display_config.should_exit_callback = display_should_exit_adapter;
181   display_config.callback_data = NULL;
182 
183   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Creating display context (before splash)");
184   display = session_display_create(&display_config);
185   if (!display) {
186     log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] session_display_create() returned NULL");
187     log_fatal("Failed to initialize display context");
188     result = ERROR_DISPLAY;
189     goto cleanup;
190   }
191   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Display context created, will use for splash");
192 
193   // Register global display context for signal handlers (Ctrl+C help screen closure)
194   // This makes the context available to signal handlers for the entire app lifetime
195   session_display_set_global_context(display);
196 
197   // ============================================================================
198   // SETUP: Splash Screen (using unified display context)
199   // ============================================================================
200 
201   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Starting splash with display context");
202 
203   // Use the display context we just created for splash animation
204   splash_intro_start(display);
205   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): splash_intro_start() returned");
206 
207   // Detect if we're using media vs webcam (needed for splash timing)
208   const char *media_url = GET_OPTION(media_url);
209   const char *media_file = GET_OPTION(media_file);
210   bool has_media = (media_url && strlen(media_url) > 0) || (media_file && strlen(media_file) > 0);
211 
212   // Show splash briefly for webcam, but skip sleep for media (which takes time anyway)
213   if (!has_media && !GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
214     APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
215     APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT(platform_sleep_ms, 250);
216   }
217   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): After splash sleep");
218 
219   // Restore stderr now that splash animation and post-splash logging are done
220   // This allows logs to appear on screen again after this point
221   splash_restore_stderr();
222 
223   // Wait for background update check to finish and show prompt if update available
224   update_banner_wait_for_check();
225   if (update_banner_has_update() && !GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
226     bool wants_update = update_banner_show_prompt(display);
227     if (wants_update) {
228       update_banner_print_instructions();
229       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
230     }
231     terminal_clear_screen();
232   }
233 
234   // ============================================================================
235   // SETUP: Terminal Logging in Snapshot Mode
236   // ============================================================================
237 
238   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): About to disable terminal logging (snapshot=%d, discovery=%d)", GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode), config->discovery != NULL);
239   // For discovery mode, skip disabling logging initially - we'll handle it after role is determined
240   // Discovery participants should show logs, only hosts should have logs disabled for rendering
241   bool should_disable_logging = !GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && (config->discovery == NULL);
242   if (should_disable_logging) {
243     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Calling log_set_terminal_output(false)...");
244     log_set_terminal_output(false);
245     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): RETURNED from log_set_terminal_output(false)");
246   } else {
247     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Skipping log disable (snapshot=%d or discovery=%d)", GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode), config->discovery != NULL);
248   }
249   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Terminal logging setup complete, starting media source setup");
250 
251   // ============================================================================
252   // SETUP: Media Source Selection and FPS Probing
253   // ============================================================================
254 
255   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Initializing capture config");
256   session_capture_config_t capture_config = {0};
257   capture_config.resize_for_network = false;
258   capture_config.should_exit_callback = capture_should_exit_adapter;
259   capture_config.callback_data = NULL;
260 
261   // Determine FPS explicitly set by user
262   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting FPS option");
263   int user_fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
264   bool fps_explicitly_set = user_fps > 0;
265   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): FPS=%d (explicitly_set=%d)", user_fps, fps_explicitly_set);
266 
267   // Select media source based on options (priority order)
268   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_url option");
269   const char *media_url_val = GET_OPTION(media_url);
270   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_file option");
271   const char *media_file_val = GET_OPTION(media_file);
272   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): media_url=%s, media_file=%s", media_url_val ? media_url_val : "(null)",
273             media_file_val ? media_file_val : "(null)");
274 
275   if (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) {
276     // Network URL takes priority
277     log_info("Using network URL: %s (webcam disabled)", media_url_val);
278     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
279     capture_config.path = media_url_val;
280     capture_config.loop = false; // Network URLs cannot be looped
281 
282     if (fps_explicitly_set) {
283       capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
284       log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
285     } else {
286       // Always probe FPS for HTTP URLs, even in snapshot mode
287       // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
288       log_debug("Probing FPS for HTTP URL");
289       probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_url_val);
290       if (probe_source) {
291         double url_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
292         log_info("Detected HTTP stream video FPS: %.1f", url_fps);
293         if (url_fps > 0.0) {
294           capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(url_fps + 0.5);
295         } else {
296           log_warn("FPS detection failed for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
297           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
298         }
299       } else {
300         log_warn("Failed to create probe source for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
301         capture_config.target_fps = 60;
302       }
303     }
304   } else if (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) {
305     // Local file or stdin
306     log_info("DEBUG: media_file_val='%s', strcmp result=%d", media_file_val, strcmp(media_file_val, "-"));
307     if (strcmp(media_file_val, "-") == 0) {
308       log_info("Using stdin for media streaming (webcam disabled)");
309       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN;
310       capture_config.path = NULL;
311       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
312       capture_config.loop = false;
313     } else {
314       log_info("Using media file: %s (webcam disabled)", media_file_val);
315       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
316       capture_config.path = media_file_val;
317       capture_config.loop = GET_OPTION(media_loop);
318 
319       if (fps_explicitly_set) {
320         capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
321         log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
322       } else {
323         // Always probe FPS for local files, even in snapshot mode
324         // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
325         log_debug("Probing FPS for local file");
326         probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_file_val);
327         if (probe_source) {
328           double file_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
329           log_info("Detected file video FPS: %.1f", file_fps);
330           if (file_fps > 0.0) {
331             capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(file_fps + 0.5);
332           } else {
333             log_warn("FPS detection failed, using default 60 FPS");
334             capture_config.target_fps = 60;
335           }
336         } else {
337           log_warn("Failed to create probe source for FPS detection, using default 60 FPS");
338           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
339         }
340       }
341     }
342   } else if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
343     // Test pattern
344     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Using test pattern");
345     log_info("Using test pattern");
346     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
347     capture_config.path = NULL;
348     capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
349     capture_config.loop = false;
350   } else {
351     // Default: Try webcam if stdout is a TTY, otherwise use test pattern
352     // (webcam + piped output often fails, test pattern is reliable fallback)
353     bool stdout_is_tty = terminal_is_stdout_tty();
354     if (stdout_is_tty) {
355       // Normal terminal mode: use webcam
356       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is TTY, using local webcam");
357       log_info("Using local webcam");
358       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM;
359       capture_config.path = NULL;
360       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
361       capture_config.loop = false;
362     } else {
363       // Piped output mode: use test pattern (webcam + pipes often fails)
364       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is piped, defaulting to test pattern for reliability");
365       log_info("Stdout is piped - using test pattern instead of webcam");
366       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
367       capture_config.path = NULL;
368       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
369       capture_config.loop = false;
370     }
371   }
372 
373   // Apply initial seek if specified
374   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_seek_timestamp option");
375   capture_config.initial_seek_timestamp = GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp);
376   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Media seek timestamp set");
377 
378   // Clean up probe source (don't reuse for actual capture)
379   if (probe_source) {
380     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Destroying probe source");
381     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
382     probe_source = NULL;
383     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Probe source destroyed");
384   } else {
385     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): No probe source to destroy");
386   }
387 
388   // Pass probed FPS to display context for correct render-file duration
389   if (capture_config.target_fps > 0) {
390     session_display_set_render_fps(display, capture_config.target_fps);
391     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Set display render FPS to %u for correct video encoding duration",
392               capture_config.target_fps);
393   }
394 
395   // ============================================================================
396   // SETUP: Network Transports (TCP/WebSocket)
397   // ============================================================================
398 
399   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up network transports");
400 
401   bool networked_mode = session_client_like_is_networked(config->kind);
402 
403   switch (config->kind) {
404     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR:
405       log_debug("Mirror mode detected - will use local capture with media source");
406       break;
407     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT:
408       log_debug("Client/Network mode detected - will use network capture without local media source");
409       break;
410     case SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY:
411       log_debug("Discovery mode detected - discovery session will manage networking");
412       break;
413   }
414 
415   // ============================================================================
416   // SETUP: Capture Context
417   // ============================================================================
418 
419   // Check for stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, render to video
420   // Only enable stdin render mode if:
421   // 1. render-file is "-" (output to stdout)
422   // 2. stdin is not a TTY
423   // 3. --height is explicitly specified (user must opt-in to stdin render mode)
424   // 4. NO media source is being used (not reading from file/URL/test-pattern)
425   // This prevents accidental activation when piping to other processes or using mirror mode
426   const char *render_file_opt = GET_OPTION(render_file);
427   int frame_height = GET_OPTION(height);
428   bool height_explicitly_set = (frame_height != OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT);
429   bool has_media_source = (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) ||
430                           (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) ||
431                           GET_OPTION(test_pattern);
432 
433   // Enable audio capture if render-file is active and media source exists
434   if (render_file_opt && strlen(render_file_opt) > 0 && has_media_source) {
435     capture_config.enable_audio = true;
436     log_debug("Audio capture enabled for render-file encoding");
437   }
438 
439   bool stdin_render_mode = (render_file_opt && strcmp(render_file_opt, "-") == 0 &&
440                             !terminal_is_stdin_tty() && height_explicitly_set &&
441                             !has_media_source && config->kind != SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR);
442 
443   if (stdin_render_mode) {
444     // Stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, output video to stdout
445     log_info("Stdin render mode enabled: reading ASCII frames from stdin, output to stdout");
446 
447     // Get frame dimensions (--height must be explicitly set to reach here)
448     int frame_width = GET_OPTION(width);
449 
450     // Validate stdin render mode constraints (--height is already validated above since height_explicitly_set is true)
451     bool width_was_explicitly_set = (frame_width != OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT);
452 
453     if (width_was_explicitly_set) {
454       result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Stdin render mode does not accept --width (auto-detected from frames).\n"
455                                       "Only specify: --height <rows>");
456       goto cleanup;
457     }
458 
459     asciichat_error_t stdin_err = terminal_fd_reader_create(STDIN_FILENO, frame_height, &g_stdin_reader);
460     if (stdin_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
461       log_fatal("Failed to initialize stdin frame reader: %s", asciichat_error_string(stdin_err));
462       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
463       goto cleanup;
464     }
465 
466     // For stdin render mode, create a minimal capture context for compatibility
467     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
468     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
469     if (!capture) {
470       log_fatal("Failed to initialize capture context for stdin rendering");
471       terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
472       g_stdin_reader = NULL;
473       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
474       goto cleanup;
475     }
476 
477     log_info("stdin render mode: reading %d-line frames from stdin, auto-detecting width", frame_height);
478   }
479 
480   // Choose capture type based on mode determined earlier:
481   // - Mirror mode: needs to capture local media (webcam, file, test pattern)
482   // - Network modes (client/discovery): receive frames from network, no local capture
483   if (!stdin_render_mode && networked_mode) {
484     // Network mode: create minimal capture context without media source
485     log_debug("Network mode detected - using network capture (no local media source)");
486     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
487     uint64_t net_cap_start = time_get_ns();
488     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
489     double net_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(net_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
490     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Network capture created in %.1f ms", net_cap_ms);
491     if (!capture) {
492       log_fatal("Failed to initialize network capture context");
493       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
494       goto cleanup;
495     }
496     if (fps > 0) {
497       log_debug("Network capture FPS set to %d from options", fps);
498     }
499   }
500 
501   // ============================================================================
502   // SETUP: Audio Context
503   // ============================================================================
504 
505   // Skip audio for immediate snapshots
506   bool should_init_audio = true;
507   if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0) {
508     should_init_audio = false;
509     log_debug("Skipping audio initialization for immediate snapshot");
510   }
511 
512   // Probe for audio
513   if (should_init_audio && capture_config.type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE && capture_config.path) {
514     media_source_t *audio_probe_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
515     if (audio_probe_source && media_source_has_audio(audio_probe_source)) {
516       audio_available = true;
517 
518       // Allocate and initialize audio context
519       audio_ctx = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(audio_context_t), audio_context_t *);
520       if (audio_ctx) {
521         *audio_ctx = (audio_context_t){0};
522         if (audio_init(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
523           // Link audio to media source
524           media_source_t *media_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
525           audio_ctx->media_source = media_source;
526 
527           if (media_source) {
528             media_source_set_audio_context(media_source, audio_ctx);
529           }
530 
531           // Determine if microphone should be enabled
532           bool should_enable_mic = audio_should_enable_microphone(GET_OPTION(audio_source), audio_available);
533           audio_ctx->playback_only = !should_enable_mic;
534 
535           // Disable jitter buffering for file playback
536           if (audio_ctx->playback_buffer) {
537             audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_enabled = false;
538             atomic_store_bool(&audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_filled, true);
539           }
540 
541           // Store in capture for keyboard handler access
542           session_capture_set_audio_context(capture, audio_ctx);
543 
544           log_debug("Audio context initialized");
545         } else {
546           log_warn("Failed to initialize audio context");
547           audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
548           SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
549           audio_ctx = NULL;
550           audio_available = false;
551         }
552       }
553     }
554   }
555 
556   // Pass stdin_reader to display if in stdin render mode
557   if (stdin_render_mode && g_stdin_reader) {
558     session_display_set_stdin_reader(display, g_stdin_reader);
559     log_debug("stdin_reader passed to display context");
560   }
561 
562   // ============================================================================
563   // SETUP: End Splash Screen
564   // ============================================================================
565   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] About to end splash screen");
566 
567   // The splash screen will display during initialization and exit when done.
568   // The initialization process (media probing, display setup, etc.) naturally
569   // takes time, keeping the splash visible. The splash is kept alive during
570   // connection attempts (TCP and WebSocket) to prevent a blank screen during
571   // network operations. splash_intro_done() is called only after a successful
572   // connection is established.
573 
574   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Splash will remain visible during connection attempts");
575 
576   // Exit early if shutdown was requested (e.g., user pressed Ctrl-C)
577   if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
578     log_debug("[SETUP] Shutdown requested, exiting early");
579     log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Ending splash due to early shutdown request");
580     splash_intro_done();
581     splash_wait_for_animation();
582     result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
583     goto cleanup;
584   }
585 
586   // Wait for splash animation to finish before proceeding to render loop
587   // (splash_anim thread uses the display, must not be disrupted before cleanup)
588   // But skip for immediate snapshots (snapshot-delay=0) which need to output first frame immediately
589   // For discovery mode, keep the splash running - it stays visible while waiting for peers
590   if (config->discovery == NULL) {
591     splash_intro_done();
592     if (!(GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0)) {
593       splash_wait_for_animation();
594     }
595   } else {
596     log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Discovery mode: keeping splash visible while waiting for peers");
597   }
598 
599   // ============================================================================
600   // SETUP: Create Mirror Capture (after splash screen finishes)
601   // ============================================================================
602 
603   log_info("[MIRROR_CHECK] stdin_render_mode=%d, capture=%p", stdin_render_mode, (void *)capture);
604   if (!stdin_render_mode && !capture) {
605     // Mirror mode: create capture context with local media source (NOW, after splash finishes)
606     log_info("Mirror mode detected - creating mirror capture after splash screen");
607     uint64_t mirror_cap_start = time_get_ns();
608     capture = session_mirror_capture_create(&capture_config);
609     double mirror_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(mirror_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
610     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Mirror capture created in %.1f ms (after splash)", mirror_cap_ms);
611     if (!capture) {
612       log_fatal("Failed to initialize mirror capture source");
613       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
614       goto cleanup;
615     }
616 
617     // Set audio source for render-file encoding (after capture is created)
618     media_source_t *render_audio_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
619     const char *render_file_path = GET_OPTION(render_file);
620     log_info("[AUDIO_SOURCE] render_audio_source=%p, render_file=%s, render_file_len=%zu",
621              render_audio_source, render_file_path ? render_file_path : "(null)",
622              render_file_path ? strlen(render_file_path) : 0);
623     if (render_audio_source && render_file_path && strlen(render_file_path) > 0) {
624       session_display_set_render_audio_source(display, render_audio_source);
625       log_info("Audio source set for render-file output");
626     } else {
627       log_warn("[AUDIO_SOURCE] Conditions not met: render_audio_source=%p, render_file_path=%s, len=%zu",
628                render_audio_source, render_file_path ? render_file_path : "(null)",
629                render_file_path ? strlen(render_file_path) : 0);
630     }
631   }
632 
633   // ============================================================================
634   // SETUP: Start Audio Playback
635   // ============================================================================
636 
637   log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] About to check audio context for duplex");
638   if (audio_ctx && audio_available) {
639     log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] Starting audio duplex");
640     if (audio_start_duplex(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
641       log_info("Audio playback started");
642     } else {
643       log_warn("Failed to start audio duplex");
644       audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
645       SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
646       audio_ctx = NULL;
647       audio_available = false;
648     }
649   }
650 
651   log_info("[MARKER] Audio setup complete at line 614");
652   log_info("[SETUP_COMPLETE] All setup complete, about to start connection loop");
653 
654   // ============================================================================
655   // RUN: Mode-Specific Main Loop with Reconnection
656   // ============================================================================
657 
658   // Connection/attempt loop - wraps run_fn with reconnection logic
659   int attempt = 0;
660   int max_attempts = config->max_reconnect_attempts;
661 
662   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_LOOP] Starting connection loop, max_attempts=%d", max_attempts);
663 
664   while (true) {
665     attempt++;
666 
667     result = config->run_fn(capture, display, config->run_user_data);
668 
669     // Exit immediately if run_fn succeeded
670     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
671       // Connection succeeded - splash screen cleanup now happens when first frame renders
672       // This ensures the splash stays visible during TCP/WebSocket/datachannel connection attempts
673 
674       break;
675     }
676 
677     // Check if we should attempt reconnection
678     bool should_retry = false;
679 
680     if (max_attempts != 0) { // max_attempts == 0 means no retries
681       // Check custom reconnect logic if provided
682       if (config->should_reconnect_callback) {
683         should_retry = config->should_reconnect_callback(result, attempt, config->reconnect_user_data);
684       } else {
685         should_retry = true; // Default: always retry
686       }
687 
688       // Check retry limits if still planning to retry
689       if (should_retry && max_attempts > 0 && attempt >= max_attempts) {
690         log_debug("Reached maximum reconnection attempts (%d), giving up", max_attempts);
691         should_retry = false;
692       }
693     }
694 
695     // If not retrying or shutdown requested, exit loop
696     if (!should_retry || APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
697       break;
698     }
699 
700     // Reconnection will happen - show splash screen during reconnection attempt
701     // Reset first_frame flag so splash cleanup runs on next successful connection
702     session_display_reset_first_frame(display);
703 
704     // splash_intro_start() handles all the logic:
705     // - If splash should display: shows splash and disables console logging
706     // - If splash shouldn't display: restores stderr and returns without changing logging state
707     splash_intro_start(display);
708 
709     // Log reconnection message
710     if (max_attempts == -1) {
711       log_info("Connection failed, retrying...");
712     } else if (max_attempts > 0) {
713       log_info("Connection failed, retrying (attempt %d/%d)...", attempt + 1, max_attempts);
714     }
715 
716     // Apply reconnection delay if configured
717     // Check APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit) frequently during sleep so SIGTERM can interrupt reconnection attempts
718     if (config->reconnect_delay_ms > 0) {
719       unsigned int remaining_ms = config->reconnect_delay_ms;
720       const unsigned int check_interval_ms = 100; // Check exit flag every 100ms
721 
722       while (remaining_ms > 0 && !APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
723         unsigned int sleep_ms = (remaining_ms < check_interval_ms) ? remaining_ms : check_interval_ms;
724         APP_CALLBACK_VOID(platform_pump_events);
725         APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT(platform_sleep_ms, sleep_ms);
726         remaining_ms -= sleep_ms;
727       }
728     }
729 
730     // Continue loop to retry
731   }
732 
733   // ============================================================================
734   // CLEANUP (always runs, even on error)
735   // ============================================================================
736 
737 cleanup:
738   // Re-enable terminal output for shutdown logs
739   log_set_terminal_output(true);
740 
741   g_current_config = NULL;
742   g_render_should_exit = NULL;
743 
744   // CRITICAL: Terminate PortAudio device resources FIRST
745   log_debug("Terminating PortAudio device resources");
746   audio_terminate_portaudio_final();
747 
748   // Stop audio thread before destroying audio context to prevent use-after-free
749   // The audio worker thread may still be logging when we destroy the buffer
750   APP_CALLBACK_VOID(audio_stop_thread);
751 
752   // Stop and destroy audio (after PortAudio is terminated and audio thread is stopped)
753   if (audio_ctx) {
754     audio_stop_duplex(audio_ctx);
755     audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
756     SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
757     audio_ctx = NULL;
758   }
759 
760   // Destroy display (check global context to see if discovery mode already destroyed it)
761   // Discovery participant mode may destroy the display early and clear the global context
762   session_display_ctx_t *current_global = session_display_get_global_context();
763   if (display && display == current_global) {
764     // Display is still valid (not destroyed by discovery mode)
765     session_display_destroy(display);
766     display = NULL;
767   } else if (display && display != current_global) {
768     // Display was already destroyed (by discovery participant mode)
769     // Just NULL it out, don't call destroy again
770     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Display was already destroyed by discovery mode, skipping destroy");
771     display = NULL;
772   }
773 
774   // Destroy capture
775   if (capture) {
776     session_capture_destroy(capture);
777     capture = NULL;
778   }
779 
780   // Destroy stdin reader if active (stdin render mode)
781   if (g_stdin_reader) {
782     terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
783     g_stdin_reader = NULL;
784   }
785 
786   // Clean up probe source if still allocated (shouldn't happen, but be safe)
787   if (probe_source) {
788     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
789     probe_source = NULL;
790   }
791 
792   // Stop splash animation and enforce minimum display time (even on error path)
793   // But skip completely if shutting down - don't interact with splash at all during shutdown
794   // The animation thread will exit on its own when it detects shutdown_is_requested()
795   // For discovery mode, keep the splash running - it stays visible while waiting for peers
796   if (config->discovery != NULL) {
797     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Discovery mode: keeping splash alive for peer waiting UI");
798   } else if (!APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
799     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_intro_done()");
800     splash_intro_done();
801     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_intro_done() returned");
802 
803     // Wait for animation thread to exit before cleanup
804     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_wait_for_animation()");
805     splash_wait_for_animation();
806     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_wait_for_animation() returned");
807   } else {
808     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Skipping all splash operations (shutdown in progress)");
809     // During shutdown, don't interact with splash - let animation thread exit naturally
810     // and don't wait for it (prevents blocking on signals)
811   }
812 
813   // Stop debug sync thread before destroying log buffer to prevent use-after-free
814   log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call debug_sync_cleanup_thread()");
815   debug_sync_cleanup_thread();
816   log_debug("[CLEANUP] debug_sync_cleanup_thread() returned");
817 
818   // Disable keepawake (re-allow OS to sleep)
819   log_debug("[CLEANUP] Disabling keepawake");
820   platform_disable_keepawake();
821   log_debug("[CLEANUP] keepawake disabled");
822 
823   // Write newline to separate final frame from prompt (if configured)
824   log_debug("About to check print_newline_on_tty_exit");
825   if (config->print_newline_on_tty_exit && terminal_is_stdout_tty()) {
826     log_debug("Writing newline");
827     const char newline = '\n';
828     (void)platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, &newline, 1);
829   }
830 
831   log_set_terminal_output(false);
832 
833   return result;
834 }




References APP_CALLBACK_BOOL, APP_CALLBACK_VOID,
    APP_CALLBACK_VOID_UINT, ASCIICHAT_OK,
    atomic_store_bool(), audio_destroy(), audio_init(),
    audio_should_enable_microphone(), audio_start_duplex(),
    audio_stop_duplex(), audio_stop_thread(),
    audio_terminate_portaudio_final(),
    session_capture_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::color_mode,
    session_display_config_t::custom_palette,
    debug_sync_cleanup_thread(),
    session_client_like_config::discovery,
    session_capture_config_t::enable_audio, ERROR_DISPLAY,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEDIA_INIT, ERROR_USAGE,
    GET_OPTION, session_capture_config_t::initial_seek_timestamp,
    audio_ring_buffer::jitter_buffer_enabled,
    audio_ring_buffer::jitter_buffer_filled,
    session_client_like_config::kind, log_debug, log_fatal,
    log_info, log_set_force_stderr(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), log_warn,
    session_capture_config_t::loop,
    session_client_like_config::max_reconnect_attempts,
    audio_context_t::media_source, media_source_create(),
    media_source_destroy(), MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE,
    media_source_get_video_fps(), media_source_has_audio(),
    media_source_set_audio_context(), MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN,
    MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST, MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM,
    OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT, OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT,
    session_display_config_t::palette_type,
    session_capture_config_t::path, platform_disable_keepawake(),
    platform_enable_keepawake(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    platform_write_all(), audio_context_t::playback_buffer,
    audio_context_t::playback_only,
    session_client_like_config::print_newline_on_tty_exit,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_delay_ms,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_user_data,
    session_capture_config_t::resize_for_network,
    session_client_like_config::run_fn,
    session_client_like_config::run_user_data, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, session_capture_destroy(),
    session_capture_get_media_source(),
    session_capture_set_audio_context(),
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT,
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_DISCOVERY,
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_MIRROR, session_display_create(),
    session_display_destroy(),
    session_display_get_global_context(),
    session_display_reset_first_frame(),
    session_display_set_global_context(),
    session_display_set_render_audio_source(),
    session_display_set_render_fps(),
    session_display_set_stdin_reader(),
    session_mirror_capture_create(),
    session_network_capture_create(), SET_ERRNO,
    should_exit(), session_capture_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_display_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_client_like_config::should_reconnect_callback,
    session_display_config_t::snapshot_mode, splash_intro_done(),
    splash_intro_start(), splash_restore_stderr(),
    splash_wait_for_animation(),
    session_capture_config_t::target_fps, TERM_COLOR_AUTO,
    terminal_clear_screen(), terminal_fd_reader_create(),
    terminal_fd_reader_destroy(), terminal_is_stdin_tty(),
    terminal_is_stdout_tty(), terminal_should_force_stderr(),
    time_elapsed_ns(), time_get_ns(),
    session_capture_config_t::type, update_banner_has_update(),
    update_banner_print_instructions(),
    update_banner_show_prompt(), and
    update_banner_wait_for_check().


Referenced by client_main(), discovery_main(), and
    mirror_main().








Variable
  Documentation




bool(*)(void *)
  session_client_like_get_render_should_exit(void) (void )


Definition at line 50 of file client_like.c.


50                                                                  {
51   return g_render_should_exit;
52 }
